description: HPLC modeled as Lock
MASS_SPEC modeled as Lock
CELL_LAB modeled as Lock
channel from CHEMIST to BIOLOGIST for compound sample
channel from CHEMIST to TOXICOLOGIST for compound sample
channel from BIOLOGIST to LEAD_SCIENTIST for bioactivity report
channel from TOXICOLOGIST to LEAD_SCIENTIST for toxicity report
channel from LEAD_SCIENTIST to REGULATORY_SPECIALIST for go/no-go decision
channel from REGULATORY_SPECIALIST to LEAD_SCIENTIST for rejection notice
channel from REGULATORY_SPECIALIST to CHEMIST for compliance approval
channel from LEAD_SCIENTIST to BIOLOGIST for additional assay request
channel from LEAD_SCIENTIST to TOXICOLOGIST for additional assay request
BIOLOGIST's bioassay can pass or fail
BIOLOGIST's HPLC stability check can pass or fail
TOXICOLOGIST's cytotoxicity assay can pass or fail
REGULATORY_SPECIALIST can approve or reject LEAD_SCIENTIST's decision
BIOLOGIST evaluates only after receiving compound from CHEMIST
TOXICOLOGIST evaluates only after receiving compound from CHEMIST
LEAD_SCIENTIST evaluates only after receiving BOTH bio and tox reports
CHEMIST documentation only after regulatory approval
run_hplc_analysis requires holding HPLC
run_mass_spec_analysis requires holding MASS_SPEC
run_cell_assay requires holding CELL_LAB
BIOLOGIST (CELL_LAB->HPLC) and TOXICOLOGIST (HPLC->CELL_LAB) acquire in opposite order
CHEMIST uses HPLC at start AND end — must release before waiting for regulatory approval
TOXICOLOGIST may hold HPLC while needing CELL_LAB — hold-and-wait
regulatory rejection loops back to LEAD_SCIENTIST, who may request additional assays — compounds retry contention
assay failures create additional instrument acquisition rounds
LEAD_SCIENTIST receives bio and tox reports in either order
BIOLOGIST and TOXICOLOGIST evaluate compound in parallel
all five scientists perform preparation work concurrently
